input: Goal: Deploy a new microservice to production. Available capabilities: git_clone, docker_build, run_tests, k8s_deploy, health_check, rollback, notify_slack. Constraints: must pass all integration tests before deployment, zero-downtime rollout required, deployment window is Saturday 2AM-4AM UTC.

expected_output.goal: Deploy a new microservice to production

steps.description: Clone the latest source code from the repository
Build the Docker image for the microservice
steps.expected_output: Docker image tagged with build version

steps.description: Run the full integration test suite
steps.expected_output: Test results report with pass/fail status

steps.description: Deploy to Kubernetes cluster with rolling update strategy
steps.expected_output: Deployment confirmed with new pods running

steps.description: Verify the health of all deployed instances
Notify the team about successful deployment via Slack
Rollback to previous version if deployment fails
steps.expected_output: System restored to previous stable version

input: Goal: Migrate a 500GB PostgreSQL database from on-premises to AWS RDS. Available capabilities: db_backup, db_restore, schema_validate, data_integrity_check, dns_switch, monitor_replication, aws_rds_create. Constraints: maximum downtime of 15 minutes, must retain all existing data, rollback plan required, compliance audit log must be generated.

expected_output.goal: Migrate a 500GB PostgreSQL database from on-premises to AWS RDS

steps.description: Create target RDS instance with matching PostgreSQL version and specs
Perform full database backup from on-premises PostgreSQL
Restore backup to the target RDS instance
Validate that the restored schema matches source schema
Run data integrity checks comparing source and target
steps.expected_output: Integrity check passing with row counts matching

steps.description: Enable replication monitoring during cutover window
Switch DNS to point to new RDS endpoint
Rollback by switching DNS back to on-premises database

input: Goal: Implement a user authentication system with OAuth2 and JWT tokens. Available capabilities: write_code, run_unit_tests, run_integration_tests, code_review, security_scan, create_docs, merge_pr. Constraints: must support Google and GitHub OAuth providers, JWT tokens must expire in 1 hour, refresh token rotation required, must pass OWASP Top 10 security scan, TypeScript with Node.js.

expected_output.goal: Implement a user authentication system with OAuth2 and JWT tokens

steps.description: Write the core OAuth2 provider abstraction layer with Google and GitHub strategies
steps.expected_output: OAuth2 provider module with Google and GitHub integrations

steps.description: Implement JWT token generation and validation with configurable expiration
Implement refresh token rotation mechanism with secure storage
Write unit tests for all authentication modules
steps.expected_output: All unit tests passing at 90%+ coverage

steps.description: Write integration tests for end-to-end OAuth flow
Run OWASP Top 10 security scan against the auth endpoints
steps.expected_output: Security scan report with zero critical/high findings

steps.description: Peer code review of the authentication module
Generate API documentation for the auth endpoints
Merge the pull request to main branch

input: Goal: Diagnose and fix a production outage where API latency has spiked from 200ms to 5000ms. Available capabilities: read_logs, query_metrics, trace_requests, check_db_connections, restart_service, scale_instances, notify_oncall. Constraints: must not cause additional downtime, root cause analysis required before any fix, incident response SLA is 30 minutes, must preserve all diagnostic data for postmortem.

expected_output.goal: Diagnose and fix a production outage where API latency has spiked from 200ms to 5000ms

steps.description: Query real-time metrics to identify which service components are experiencing latency
Check database connection pool status and slow query logs
steps.expected_output: DB connection pool statistics and slow queries

steps.description: Trace a sample of slow requests to pinpoint the exact bottleneck
Analyze application and infrastructure logs for error patterns
Perform root cause analysis synthesizing all diagnostic data
steps.expected_output: Root cause identified with high confidence

steps.description: Scale up service instances to handle increased load during investigation
Apply targeted fix based on root cause analysis
Notify on-call engineer if automated fix fails

input: Goal: Design a comprehensive onboarding flow for a SaaS platform. Available capabilities: user_research, wireframe, prototype, user_test, iterate_design, implement_ui, a11y_audit, analytics_setup. Constraints: must support web and mobile, accessibility WCAG 2.1 AA compliance required, user testing with at least 5 participants, must integrate with existing design system, timeline is 4 weeks.

expected_output.goal: Design a comprehensive onboarding flow for a SaaS platform

steps.description: Conduct user research to understand new user needs and pain points
steps.expected_output: User research report with key insights

steps.description: Create low-fidelity wireframes for onboarding flow on web and mobile
Build interactive prototype of the onboarding flow
steps.expected_output: Clickable prototype for web and mobile

steps.description: Conduct usability testing with 5+ participants
Iterate design based on usability test feedback
Implement the onboarding UI with the design system components
Run accessibility audit to ensure WCAG 2.1 AA compliance
Set up analytics tracking for onboarding funnel metrics

input: Goal: Write a blog post about the benefits of remote work. Execution plan: [S1: Research statistics on remote work] → [S2: Draft the blog post] → [S3: Review and edit] → [S4: Publish]. Execution results: S1 found 15 relevant statistics, S2 produced a 1200-word draft, S3 identified 3 grammar errors and suggested restructuring, S4 published successfully with 500 views in first day. Previous reflections: none.

expected_output.summary: The blog post was successfully researched, drafted, edited, and published. All steps completed without major issues. The post received 500 views on the first day, indicating good initial engagement.

successes.description: Found 15 relevant statistics providing strong data backing for the post
Produced a well-structured 1200-word draft that covered all key points
Publication was smooth with 500 views on day one

failures.description: 3 grammar errors were found in the draft
failures.root_cause: Rushed drafting phase without grammar checking tool

improvements.suggestion: Integrate a grammar checking tool during the drafting phase to catch errors earlier
Add a step for SEO optimization before publishing to improve search visibility

lessons_learned: Pre-research with statistics improves content quality significantly
Integrating quality checks during drafting reduces editing time

input: Goal: Renovate the kitchen within a budget of $15,000. Execution plan: [S1: Get quotes from 3 contractors] → [S2: Select contractor and sign contract] → [S3: Demolish old kitchen] → [S4: Install new cabinets and countertops] → [S5: Install new appliances] → [S6: Final inspection]. Execution results: S1 got quotes: $12K, $14.5K, $18K. S2 selected the $14.5K contractor. S3 demolition completed on time. S4 delayed by 1 week due to cabinet delivery issues. S5 appliances installed but the refrigerator was the wrong model. S6 passed inspection but final cost was $16,200 due to change orders. Previous reflections: [Previous kitchen projects had budget overruns due to scope creep].

expected_output.summary: The kitchen renovation was completed and passed inspection, but exceeded budget by $1,200 and experienced a 1-week delay. The outcome was acceptable but the process had preventable issues with supplier coordination and scope management.

successes.description: Selected a competitive bid within budget range from 3 qualified contractors
Demolition completed on schedule without issues
Final inspection passed on first attempt

failures.description: Cabinet delivery delayed by 1 week causing schedule slip
failures.root_cause: No buffer time planned for supplier delays in the schedule
Order verification step was missing — model number not confirmed before delivery

failures.description: Budget exceeded by $1,200 due to change orders
failures.root_cause: Scope creep from undocumented change requests during installation

improvements.suggestion: Add 1-week buffer to the schedule for supplier-related delays
Add a model number verification step before appliance delivery
Implement a formal change order process requiring written approval for any scope changes
Include a 10% contingency line item in the initial budget

lessons_learned: Supplier delivery timelines are unreliable — always build in buffer time
Never assume delivered items match the order — verify model numbers on arrival
Budget overruns on renovation projects are nearly always caused by undocumented scope changes
Historical patterns of scope creep should trigger proactive budget controls

input: Goal: Launch a new mobile app feature for 2 million users. Execution plan: [S1: Develop feature] → [S2: QA testing] → [S3: Beta release to 1% of users] → [S4: Monitor metrics for 48 hours] → [S5: Full rollout to 100% of users]. Execution results: S1 development completed in 3 weeks. S2 found 12 bugs, 10 fixed, 2 marked as known issues. S3 beta released to 20,000 users. S4 monitoring showed 15% crash rate increase and 30% drop in session duration. S5 rollout was paused by engineering lead. Previous reflections: [Previous feature launch had similar crash rate issues due to insufficient testing on older devices].

expected_output.summary: The feature launch was paused after beta monitoring revealed a 15% crash rate increase and 30% drop in session duration. The goal was not achieved — the feature was not rolled out to all users. Critical issues were identified that must be resolved before proceeding.

successes.description: Feature was developed and delivered within the 3-week timeline
QA testing identified 12 bugs and 10 were fixed before beta
Beta was limited to 1% of users, preventing widespread impact of the issues

failures.description: 15% crash rate increase observed during beta monitoring
failures.root_cause: Insufficient testing coverage on older device models and OS versions — same pattern as previous launch

failures.description: 30% drop in session duration indicating poor user experience
failures.root_cause: UX regression likely caused by the new feature flow being confusing or slow

failures.description: 2 bugs were marked as known issues and shipped to beta
failures.root_cause: Known issues were not properly risk-assessed before beta deployment

improvements.suggestion: Expand test device matrix to include older devices and OS versions
Add performance benchmarking as a QA gate before beta release
Require a formal risk assessment for all known issues before beta approval
Implement a staged beta with increasing percentages (0.1%, 1%, 5%, 25%)

lessons_learned: Previous launch patterns were not adequately addressed — the same crash rate issue recurred
Known issues must never be dismissed without quantitative risk assessment
A 1% beta can still affect 20,000 users — consider even smaller initial cohorts
Session duration drop is an early warning signal that should trigger automatic rollback

input: Goal: Refactor a 50,000-line monolithic codebase into microservices. Execution plan: [S1: Analyze codebase dependencies] → [S2: Design service boundaries] → [S3: Extract first service (User Service)] → [S4: Migration testing] → [S5: Deploy and monitor]. Execution results: S1 identified 200+ cross-cutting dependencies. S2 proposed 8 microservices. S3 extracted User Service but introduced 3 new API integration bugs. S4 testing revealed 15% performance regression. S5 deployment caused 2 hours of partial downtime affecting 10,000 users. Previous reflections: [Previous attempt at microservice extraction failed due to underestimated coupling].

expected_output.summary: The microservice extraction partially succeeded — the User Service was extracted but caused performance regression, integration bugs, and 2 hours of downtime affecting 10,000 users. The underlying coupling issues from previous attempts were not adequately addressed.

successes.description: Successfully identified 200+ cross-cutting dependencies and proposed a clear service boundary design
The User Service was successfully extracted as a standalone deployable unit

failures.description: 3 new API integration bugs introduced during extraction
failures.root_cause: Contract testing was not performed between the new service and remaining monolith

failures.description: 15% performance regression discovered during migration testing
failures.root_cause: Network latency from inter-service calls was not accounted for — the monolith had in-process calls

failures.description: 2 hours of partial downtime affecting 10,000 users
failures.root_cause: Deployment orchestration was not properly sequenced — database migration and service deployment happened in wrong order

improvements.suggestion: Implement contract testing between the extracted service and remaining monolith before deployment
Add performance benchmarks comparing monolith vs. microservice response times as a gate
Create a detailed deployment runbook with exact sequencing of database migrations and service deployments
Start extraction with the least-coupled module before tackling the User Service

lessons_learned: Inter-service network latency must be benchmarked and factored into performance expectations
Contract testing is non-negotiable when splitting a monolith
Deployment sequencing is as critical as the code changes themselves
Starting with the most coupled service (User) was a mistake — begin with the least coupled

input: Goal: Organize an international academic conference with 500 attendees. Execution plan: [S1: Secure venue and date] → [S2: Issue call for papers] → [S3: Peer review submissions] → [S4: Notify authors and publish program] → [S5: Handle registration and logistics] → [S6: Execute conference]. Execution results: S1 venue secured but construction next door caused noise issues. S2 received 180 submissions. S3 reviews completed with 40% acceptance rate but 3 reviewers dropped out mid-process. S4 program published 2 weeks late. S5 registration reached 450 attendees but 50 cancelled last minute due to travel restrictions. S6 conference ran but 3 sessions were cancelled due to speaker no-shows and the Wi-Fi failed for 2 hours on day 1. Previous reflections: [Previous conference had speaker no-show issues and recommended overbooking speakers].

expected_output.summary: The conference was executed with 450 attendees, but faced multiple operational issues including venue noise, reviewer dropout, late program publication, speaker no-shows, and Wi-Fi failure. The event was ultimately successful but the execution quality was significantly below expectations.

successes.description: Received 180 high-quality submissions exceeding the target of 150
Peer review completed with a well-calibrated 40% acceptance rate despite reviewer dropouts
Achieved 90% of target attendance despite last-minute cancellations

failures.description: Construction noise at venue disrupted sessions
failures.root_cause: Venue inspection did not check for nearby construction schedules

failures.description: 3 reviewers dropped out during the review process
failures.root_cause: No backup reviewer pool was established — previous reflection's recommendation was not implemented
Reviewer dropout cascaded into delayed notification timeline

failures.description: 3 sessions cancelled due to speaker no-shows
failures.root_cause: Previous reflection's recommendation to overbook speakers was not implemented

failures.description: Wi-Fi failed for 2 hours on day 1
failures.root_cause: No backup internet connection was arranged and the venue's SLA was not verified

improvements.suggestion: Add venue inspection checklist item for nearby construction and environmental noise
Recruit a backup reviewer pool of at least 20% of primary reviewers
Overbook speakers by 20% to account for no-shows
Arrange a backup 5G hotspot solution and verify venue internet SLA in contract
Build a 2-week buffer into the program publication timeline

lessons_learned: Previous reflection recommendations must be tracked and implemented — ignoring them led to repeated failures
Venue selection criteria must include environmental factors beyond the room itself
Single points of failure (Wi-Fi, reviewers) need redundancy planning
Attendee cancellation rates for international events are unpredictable — budget for 10-15% cancellation
